nonFinancialBenefits: Provision of free legal aid, medical treatment, professional counseling, and temporary shelter through Sakhi centers.
maximumBenefitAmount: Amount as per latest government notification
benefitFrequency: Assistance is provided on a case-to-case basis depending on the survivor's immediate and long-term needs.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the Aadhaar card for identification purposes. | Proof of residence such as a voter ID card or ration card. | Photocopy of the domestic incident report or first information report if already filed. | Medical certificate or injury reports from a government hospital if applicable. | Recent passport size photographs of the victim for official records. | Bank account details for the transfer of potential financial assistance.
modeOfApplication: The application can be made offline by visiting a protection officer or online via the official departmental portal.
stepByStepProcess: The victim or their representative must approach the nearest Protection Officer or registered Service Provider. | A Domestic Incident Report is prepared by the officer based on the detailed statement provided by the complainant. | The Protection Officer facilitates immediate medical examination at a government facility if physical injuries are reported. | Counseling sessions are organized for the involved parties to explore the possibility of reconciliation or mediation. | If the matter requires judicial intervention the case is formally forwarded to the competent Magistrate for hearing. | The victim is provided with access to temporary shelter and rehabilitation support during the legal proceedings.
processingTime: Immediate intervention is provided with legal proceedings following the standard court timeline.
approvalAuthority: District Program Officer of the Women and Child Development Department.
importantDates: The scheme is currently active and accepts applications on a continuous basis.

overview: The One Stop Center scheme also known as Sakhi is a central sector initiative implemented through the Women and Child Development Department of Madhya Pradesh. It provides a comprehensive range of support services to women affected by violence in both private and public spheres including domestic violence and sexual harassment. The centers are established across various districts to ensure that victims have immediate access to specialized assistance under a single roof. This initiative aims to reduce the trauma experienced by women by streamlining the response mechanism and providing a safe environment for recovery. The centers function twenty four hours a day to provide emergency response and rescue services to any woman in distress. Each center is equipped with trained professionals including counselors and legal experts to handle sensitive cases with confidentiality and empathy.
objectives: The primary objective of the scheme is to provide integrated support and assistance to women affected by violence under one roof to facilitate their recovery and rehabilitation. It aims to offer immediate access to medical aid, police assistance, legal counseling, and psychosocial support to ensure the safety and well-being of the victim. The scheme also seeks to establish a coordinated mechanism for the protection of women's rights and to provide temporary shelter to those in need. By centralizing these services the department ensures that victims do not have to navigate multiple government offices during a period of crisis.
targetBeneficiaries: The target beneficiaries include all women regardless of their age, class, caste, religion, marital status, or geographical location who are facing any form of violence or distress. This includes victims of domestic violence, sexual assault, trafficking, and social exclusion who require immediate protection and support services.
ageCriteria: There is no specific age bar for women seeking assistance as the scheme is open to all female victims including girls below eighteen years of age.
incomeCriteria: The scheme does not impose any income limits and is accessible to all women regardless of their economic background or financial status.
residencyCriteria: Any woman present within the jurisdiction of Madhya Pradesh who is in distress can approach the center regardless of her permanent residence.
categoryCriteria: The benefits are available to women from all social categories and no specific caste or category certification is required for emergency assistance.
occupationCriteria: The scheme is open to all women irrespective of whether they are employed, self-employed, students, or homemakers.
otherConditions: The primary condition for seeking help is that the individual must be a victim of violence or in a state of distress requiring government intervention.
financialBenefits: There are no direct cash transfers to the beneficiaries but all services including medical care and legal aid are provided free of cost by the state.
nonFinancialBenefits: Beneficiaries receive emergency medical assistance, police facilitation, legal counseling, and psychological support along with temporary shelter facilities for up to five days.
maximumBenefitAmount: Amount as per latest government notification
benefitFrequency: Services are provided as and when required by the victim based on the nature and severity of the incident.
requiredDocuments: A copy of the Aadhar card or any other valid government identification is preferred but not mandatory for emergency rescue. | The victim may provide any existing police complaints or First Information Reports related to the incident if available. | Medical reports from previous checkups can be submitted to assist the medical team at the center. | Contact details of family members or trusted individuals may be shared for coordination purposes. | Proof of residence such as a Voter ID or Ration Card may be requested for long term rehabilitation planning. | Photographs of the victim or the site of the incident can be provided as evidence for legal proceedings.
modeOfApplication: The application process can be initiated through physical walk-in, phone call to the helpline, or referral by the police or community members.
stepByStepProcess: The victim or a representative contacts the nearest One Stop Center or calls the Women Helpline number 181 for assistance. | A case worker at the center records the initial details and registers the case in the integrated management system. | Immediate medical examination is conducted if the victim has sustained physical injuries or requires urgent clinical care. | The center facilitates the filing of a police report or an FIR through the dedicated police desk located within the premises. | Psychosocial counseling sessions are initiated to help the victim cope with trauma and provide emotional stability. | Legal aid is provided by a qualified advocate to inform the victim about her rights and the judicial process. | Temporary shelter is arranged for the victim if she is unable to return home due to safety concerns.
processingTime: Emergency services are provided immediately upon contact while legal and rehabilitation processes follow judicial timelines.
approvalAuthority: The District Program Officer of the Women and Child Development Department oversees the functioning and case approvals at the center.
importantDates: The centers operate twenty four hours a day throughout the year to provide uninterrupted support.

overview: The Beti Bachao Beti Padhao scheme is a flagship social campaign of the government designed to address the declining child sex ratio and related issues of women empowerment. In Madhya Pradesh the Department of Women and Child Development implements this initiative through intensive community mobilization and multi-sectoral action in identified districts with low sex ratios. The program aims to change the societal mindset towards the birth and education of girl children by creating a supportive environment for their growth and development. The initiative integrates the efforts of several departments including health education and social welfare to provide a holistic support system for girls from birth through their educational journey. It focuses on enforcing existing laws against female foeticide while simultaneously promoting the importance of quality education and gender equality at the grassroots level. By leveraging local media and community leaders the scheme seeks to eradicate discriminatory practices and ensure every girl child is celebrated and provided with equal opportunities.
objectives: The primary objective of the scheme is to prevent gender-biased sex-selective elimination by strictly implementing the Pre-Conception and Pre-Natal Diagnostic Techniques Act. It also seeks to ensure the survival and protection of the girl child while promoting her education and active participation in society. Another core objective is to challenge gender stereotypes and foster a cultural shift that values girls as equal members of the community through sustained awareness campaigns and district-level interventions. The scheme aims to improve the Child Sex Ratio in the state and bridge the gender gap in secondary and higher education.
targetBeneficiaries: The target beneficiaries include girls from birth up to eighteen years of age along with expectant and lactating mothers and families residing in the state of Madhya Pradesh.
ageCriteria: The scheme primarily covers girl children from birth until they reach the age of legal adulthood or complete their higher secondary education.
incomeCriteria: There is no specific income limit for general awareness and advocacy benefits as the scheme is intended for all sections of society.
residencyCriteria: Beneficiaries must be permanent residents of Madhya Pradesh and should reside in the districts where the scheme is being actively implemented.
categoryCriteria: The scheme is inclusive and available to all categories including General Scheduled Castes Scheduled Tribes and Other Backward Classes.
otherConditions: The girl child should ideally be enrolled in a recognized school and the family must adhere to the legal requirements regarding birth registration.
financialBenefits: The scheme provides indirect financial support through scholarships and linked state initiatives such as the Ladli Laxmi Yojana for educational and health needs.
nonFinancialBenefits: Benefits include improved access to institutional delivery services priority in school admissions and participation in life skills training and empowerment workshops.
maximumBenefitAmount: Amount as per latest government notification
benefitFrequency: Benefits are distributed periodically at key stages such as birth school enrollment and completion of secondary education.
requiredDocuments: A valid birth certificate of the girl child issued by the municipal or village authority is required for registration. | The Aadhaar card of the girl child or the parents must be provided as primary proof of identity. | A domicile certificate of Madhya Pradesh is necessary to establish permanent residency within the state. | The ration card or a recent utility bill is needed for current address verification. | Passport sized photographs of the girl child and her legal guardians are required for the application records. | School enrollment certificates or bonafide student certificates are required for education related benefits.
modeOfApplication: The application and registration process is primarily conducted through local Anganwadi centers and the district offices of the Women and Child Development Department.
stepByStepProcess: The parents or guardians must visit the nearest Anganwadi center or the Office of the District Program Officer. | Applicants should obtain the official registration form for the scheme from the department office or the district portal. | All required personal details including the name of the child and parental information must be filled accurately in the form. | The completed application form must be submitted along with self-attested copies of the birth certificate and residence proof. | The designated Anganwadi worker or supervisor will conduct a preliminary verification of the submitted documents. | The verified application is then forwarded to the District Women and Child Development office for official processing and approval. | Upon successful registration the beneficiaries are notified and enrolled in the relevant district level empowerment programs.
processingTime: The registration and verification process is typically completed within thirty working days from the date of submission.
approvalAuthority: The District Collector and the District Program Officer of the Women and Child Development Department are the primary approval authorities.
importantDates: The scheme remains active throughout the year and allows for continuous registration of new beneficiaries without a fixed deadline.

overview: Kishori Shakti Yojana is a specialized initiative implemented by the Madhya Pradesh State Government under the Integrated Child Development Services framework. The scheme is specifically designed to address the nutritional health and developmental needs of adolescent girls between the ages of 11 and 18 years. It focuses on empowering young women who are often out of school and lack access to formal resources for personal growth. The program is delivered through the network of Anganwadi Centers which provide a localized platform for health interventions and skill building activities. This grassroots approach ensures that the benefits of the scheme reach the most vulnerable sections of society including rural and urban poor populations. The scheme represents a commitment to improving the social and economic status of young girls by providing them with the necessary tools for a better future. By focusing on the transition from childhood to adulthood the government aims to break the cycle of malnutrition and gender based inequality. The intervention includes nutritional support regular health monitoring and the dissemination of critical life skills that help in building self confidence and decision making abilities among participants.
objectives: The primary objective of the scheme is to improve the nutritional and health status of adolescent girls while promoting awareness about hygiene and reproductive health. It aims to provide life skills and vocational training to help girls become self reliant and socially empowered. The scheme also encourages out of school girls to re enter the formal education system or gain practical skills for future employment. Another key goal is to educate participants on environmental sanitation and family welfare practices. By fostering a supportive environment at the community level the scheme seeks to enhance the overall quality of life for young women in the state.
targetBeneficiaries: Adolescent girls in the age group of 11 to 18 years residing in Madhya Pradesh particularly those belonging to families below the poverty line and those who have dropped out of school.
ageCriteria: The applicant must be between 11 and 18 years of age.
incomeCriteria: Preference is given to girls belonging to families listed under the Below Poverty Line category or those with low annual household income.
residencyCriteria: The applicant must be a permanent resident of Madhya Pradesh.
categoryCriteria: The scheme is open to girls from all social categories including General Scheduled Castes Scheduled Tribes and Other Backward Classes.
occupationCriteria: The scheme primarily targets girls who are currently out of school or have discontinued their formal education.
otherConditions: The beneficiary must be registered with her local Anganwadi Center to avail the various services and training modules offered under the scheme.
financialBenefits: The scheme provides financial assistance for vocational training programs and skill development activities conducted by authorized training partners.
nonFinancialBenefits: Beneficiaries receive supplementary nutrition regular health checkups iron and folic acid supplementation and training in life skills and home management.
maximumBenefitAmount: Amount as per latest government notification
benefitFrequency: Benefits are provided at regular intervals throughout the duration of the girls enrollment in the scheme.
requiredDocuments: Aadhaar Card of the applicant girl for identity verification. | Valid Residence Certificate or Domicile of Madhya Pradesh. | Birth Certificate or school mark sheet as proof of age. | Below Poverty Line Ration Card or Family Income Certificate. | Recent passport size photographs for registration records. | Bank account passbook for the transfer of training related allowances.
modeOfApplication: Offline through the local Anganwadi Center.
stepByStepProcess: The interested girl or her guardian must visit the nearest Anganwadi Center in their locality. | The applicant needs to request the registration form for the Kishori Shakti Yojana from the Anganwadi Worker. | All required personal and family details must be filled accurately in the application form. | The applicant must attach photocopies of all the necessary documents mentioned in the requirements list. | The completed application form along with the documents is submitted to the Anganwadi Worker for initial verification. | The Anganwadi Worker verifies the eligibility of the candidate based on age and socioeconomic status. | Once the verification is complete the details are entered into the department database for service delivery. | The beneficiary is then assigned to a specific group for nutrition health services and training modules.
processingTime: Not officially specified
approvalAuthority: District Program Officer of the Women and Child Development Department.
importantDates: The registration remains open throughout the year at Anganwadi Centers.

overview: The Working Women Hostel scheme is a significant initiative by the Madhya Pradesh Government aimed at providing safe and affordable accommodation to working women in urban and rural areas. This scheme facilitates women who are required to reside away from their native places due to employment or professional training requirements. The hostels provide basic amenities including lodging and in some cases boarding facilities to ensure a secure environment for female professionals and trainees. The scheme is implemented through various agencies including non governmental organizations local bodies and state government departments to support the mobility of women in the workforce. By offering subsidized and regulated housing options the state government seeks to empower women and encourage their active participation in the economic development of Madhya Pradesh. These hostels are strategically located near industrial hubs and educational centers to minimize travel time and enhance the safety of the residents. The management of these facilities focuses on maintaining a disciplined yet supportive atmosphere for all occupants regardless of their socio economic background.
objectives: The primary objective of the scheme is to provide safe and conveniently located accommodation for working women who are single widowed divorced separated or married but whose families do not reside in the same city. It also aims to offer housing facilities for women undergoing training for employment or pursuing higher education in specialized fields. Furthermore the scheme seeks to reduce the financial burden on working women by providing low cost housing alternatives in cities where rental prices are otherwise prohibitive. It promotes a conducive environment for career growth and skill development by ensuring that housing insecurity does not become a barrier to professional advancement for women in the state.
targetBeneficiaries: The scheme targets working women with limited income trainees preparing for jobs and female students pursuing professional courses away from their permanent residences.
ageCriteria: The applicant must be above eighteen years of age to reside independently in the hostel facility.
incomeCriteria: The monthly gross income of the applicant should not exceed fifty thousand rupees in metropolitan cities or thirty five thousand rupees in other locations.
residencyCriteria: Preference is given to permanent residents of Madhya Pradesh who are working or studying in a city different from their hometown.
categoryCriteria: The scheme is open to all categories of women including those from scheduled castes scheduled tribes and other backward classes.
occupationCriteria: Applicants must be employed in the organized or unorganized sector or be enrolled in a recognized training program or higher education course.
otherConditions: Children up to the age of eighteen years for girls and five years for boys may stay with their mothers in the hostel.
financialBenefits: The scheme provides accommodation at highly subsidized rental rates which are significantly lower than market prices in urban centers.
nonFinancialBenefits: Residents receive a secure living environment with amenities like water supply electricity common rooms and security personnel.
maximumBenefitAmount: Amount as per latest government notification
benefitFrequency: The benefit of subsidized accommodation is provided on a monthly basis throughout the period of residency.
requiredDocuments: A valid Aadhaar card of the applicant must be submitted for identity and address verification | A certified copy of the appointment letter or employment certificate from the current employer is required | An official income certificate issued by the competent authority must be provided to verify eligibility | Recent passport size photographs of the applicant are needed for the registration process | A character certificate or reference from the previous institution or employer may be requested | A self declaration form regarding the marital status and residential address of the applicant must be attached
modeOfApplication: The application can be submitted through the offline mode at the District Women and Child Development office or directly at the hostel office.
stepByStepProcess: Visit the official website of the Women and Child Development Department of Madhya Pradesh to download the application form | Fill the application form with accurate personal professional and contact details as requested | Attach the required documents including income proof and employment certificate to the completed form | Submit the application form along with the documents to the District Program Officer or the hostel warden | Wait for the verification process where the department officials will scrutinize the submitted details and documents | Attend an interview or meeting if requested by the hostel management committee for final selection | Receive the allotment letter and pay the initial security deposit and first month rent to secure the room | Complete the joining formalities and move into the hostel as per the specified date in the allotment order
processingTime: Usually within fifteen to thirty days from the date of submission of a complete application.
approvalAuthority: The District Program Officer of the Women and Child Development Department acts as the primary approval authority.
importantDates: Applications are generally accepted throughout the year based on the availability of seats in the hostels.

overview: The Mahila Shakti Kendra scheme is a significant initiative aimed at empowering rural women through community participation and providing them with an interface for approaching the government for getting their entitlements. This scheme is implemented under the umbrella of Mission Shakti to improve the overall quality of life for women in rural areas of Madhya Pradesh. The program functions through a multi-tier structure involving state, district, and block levels to ensure effective delivery of services. It utilizes student volunteers to raise awareness about various government schemes and social issues affecting women. This creates a bridge between the government and the female population to ensure that benefits reach the grassroot level.
objectives: The primary objective of the scheme is to empower rural women by providing them with opportunities for skill development, digital literacy, and health awareness. It seeks to converge various departmental programs and services to ensure comprehensive development of women across the state. The scheme also aims to facilitate the implementation of the Beti Bachao Beti Padhao initiative and address issues related to gender based violence and discrimination. It focuses on creating an environment where women can access institutional support and gain financial independence through informed decision making.
targetBeneficiaries: Rural women across Madhya Pradesh with a special focus on those residing in backward blocks and aspirational districts.
ageCriteria: There is no specific age bar for beneficiaries but preference is given to adolescent girls and adult women residing in rural areas.
incomeCriteria: Priority is given to women belonging to families below the poverty line or those from economically weaker sections.
residencyCriteria: The applicant must be a permanent resident of Madhya Pradesh specifically residing in the designated rural blocks or districts where the scheme is active.
categoryCriteria: The scheme is open to women of all categories including general, scheduled castes, scheduled tribes, and other backward classes.
occupationCriteria: Priority is accorded to unemployed women, daily wage laborers, and those involved in small scale agricultural activities.
otherConditions: Participation in community awareness programs and willingness to engage with local student volunteers is often required for active beneficiaries.
financialBenefits: The scheme does not provide direct cash transfers but facilitates access to financial assistance through other converged government programs.
nonFinancialBenefits: Benefits include access to digital literacy training, skill enhancement programs, health counseling, and information about legal rights.
maximumBenefitAmount: Amount as per latest government notification
benefitFrequency: The support and awareness services are provided on a continuous basis throughout the duration of the scheme implementation.
requiredDocuments: Resident certificate of Madhya Pradesh is necessary to verify the domicile status of the beneficiary. | Aadhar card is required for identity verification and linking with the beneficiary database. | Bank account details are needed for receiving any indirect financial benefits or subsidies. | Caste certificate must be provided if the beneficiary belongs to a reserved category for priority consideration. | Income certificate is required to establish eligibility under the economically weaker section category. | Passport size photographs are needed for the registration process and issuing of any identity cards.
modeOfApplication: The application process is primarily offline through local block level offices or via community volunteers.
stepByStepProcess: The applicant should visit the nearest Anganwadi center or the District Level Centre for Women. | Beneficiaries must interact with the student volunteers or block level coordinators to understand the available services. | The applicant needs to fill out the registration form provided by the departmental staff at the center. | All required supporting documents should be attached to the completed application form. | The completed application must be submitted to the designated officer at the Mahila Shakti Kendra or block office. | A receipt or acknowledgment will be issued by the department after the successful submission of the application. | The department officials will verify the details provided in the application and the supporting documents.
processingTime: Approximately thirty to forty five days depending on the nature of the specific service requested.
approvalAuthority: District Magistrate or the District Program Officer of the Women and Child Development Department.
importantDates: The scheme remains active throughout the financial year as per the state government directives.

overview:
The Mukhyamantri Medhavi Vidyarthi Yojana is a flagship scholarship scheme initiated by the Government of Madhya Pradesh to support meritorious students in pursuing higher education. The scheme aims to remove financial barriers for bright students who have excelled in their 12th standard examinations, enabling them to gain admission into undergraduate programs at esteemed government and private institutions across the country.

Under this scheme, the state government bears the full tuition fee for eligible students for the entire duration of their course. This financial assistance covers a wide range of graduate-level courses in fields such as engineering, medicine, law, and general studies. The primary objective is to ensure that no talented student from the state is denied the opportunity for higher learning due to their family's economic condition, thereby fostering academic excellence and skilled human resource development within Madhya Pradesh.
objectives:
The primary objective of the Mukhyamantri Medhavi Vidyarthi Yojana is to provide financial assistance to meritorious students of Madhya Pradesh for their higher education. The scheme endeavors to ensure that students with high academic performance are not hindered by financial constraints from pursuing graduate-level courses in recognized institutions.

It aims to encourage academic excellence among students at the higher secondary level and to create a more inclusive and equitable educational ecosystem. By covering the full course fees, the government seeks to empower students to choose courses and institutions based on their aptitude and interest, thereby contributing to the development of a knowledgeable and skilled workforce for the state and the nation.
targetBeneficiaries: The scheme targets meritorious students who are bonafide residents of Madhya Pradesh and have passed their 12th standard examination from either the Madhya Pradesh Board of Secondary Education (MPBSE) or the Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) / Indian Certificate of Secondary Education (ICSE). It is specifically for students seeking admission to undergraduate courses in recognized government and private universities and colleges.
ageCriteria: Not officially specified
incomeCriteria: The annual income of the student's parents or guardian from all sources should not exceed INR 6,00,000. This income limit is applicable for all categories of applicants.
residencyCriteria: The applicant must be a bonafide resident of the state of Madhya Pradesh.
categoryCriteria: The scheme is open to students from all categories, including General, Scheduled Castes (SC), Scheduled Tribes (ST), and Other Backward Classes (OBC), provided they meet all other eligibility conditions.
otherConditions: Students must have secured a minimum of 70% marks in the 12th standard examination conducted by the MPBSE, or a minimum of 85% marks if they have passed from CBSE/ICSE boards. Additionally, students who have secured admission to eligible courses based on their ranks in national entrance examinations like JEE Mains, NEET, or CLAT are also eligible under specific rank criteria defined by the government.
financialBenefits: The government provides full financial assistance to cover the admission fee and the entire tuition fee for the complete duration of the approved undergraduate course. The amount is directly transferred to the bank account of the educational institution.
nonFinancialBenefits: The scheme provides students with the opportunity to pursue higher education in premier institutions without financial burden. This enables them to focus on their studies, enhance their career prospects, and contribute to the socio-economic development of the state.
maximumBenefitAmount: Amount as per latest government notification
benefitFrequency: The financial benefit is disbursed on an annual basis for the duration of the course, subject to the student maintaining the required academic performance and attendance.
requiredDocuments: Aadhaar card of the applicant is mandatory for e-KYC and identity verification. | The Samagra ID of the applicant's family is required for registration on the portal. | A valid Domicile Certificate of Madhya Pradesh to prove residency is necessary. | An official Income Certificate of the parents or guardian issued by a competent authority. | Mark sheets of the 10th and 12th standard board examinations. | The admission letter or proof of admission provided by the educational institution. | A copy of the bank passbook showing the applicant's account number and IFSC code. | A recent passport-sized photograph of the applicant.
modeOfApplication: Applications for the scheme are accepted online through the State Scholarship Portal 2.0 of the Government of Madhya Pradesh.
stepByStepProcess: Visit the official State Scholarship Portal 2.0 of Madhya Pradesh. | Register as a new user by providing essential details such as Aadhaar number and Samagra ID. | Log in to the portal using the credentials created during registration. | Select the Mukhyamantri Medhavi Vidyarthi Yojana from the list of available scholarship schemes. | Fill out the online application form with accurate personal, academic, and institutional details. | Upload scanned copies of all the required documents in the specified format and size. | Carefully review the entire application form for any errors before final submission. | Submit the completed application form and take a printout for your records and future reference.
processingTime: The processing and verification time varies, as it depends on the validation by the concerned educational institution and final approval by the Department of Higher Education.
approvalAuthority: The Department of Higher Education, Government of Madhya Pradesh, is the final sanctioning authority for the scheme benefits.
importantDates: The application window typically opens after the declaration of 12th standard board results and during the admission period for higher education courses. Specific dates are announced annually on the official portal.

overview:
The Mukhyamantri Scooty Yojana is a flagship initiative by the Government of Madhya Pradesh aimed at promoting academic excellence and facilitating higher education for meritorious students. Under this scheme, the top-ranking male and female students from each government higher secondary school, based on their performance in the Class 12 board examinations, are provided with a scooty. This initiative serves as a reward for their hard work and dedication, encouraging them to continue their educational journey without being hindered by transportation challenges.

The scheme not only recognizes individual student achievement but also fosters a competitive academic environment across the state's government schools. By providing a personal vehicle, the government aims to enhance student mobility, particularly for those residing in rural and remote areas, thereby improving their access to colleges and other higher learning institutions. The provision of e-scooties also aligns with the state's commitment to promoting environmentally sustainable transportation solutions.
objectives:
The primary objective of the scheme is to honor and incentivize meritorious students who excel in the Class 12 board examinations. It aims to motivate students to strive for academic distinction by offering a tangible and highly useful reward. The scheme is designed to reduce the dropout rate after secondary education by addressing the logistical barriers students face when commuting to higher education centers.

Furthermore, the policy seeks to ensure gender equality in educational opportunities by providing scooties to both the highest-scoring male and female students in every eligible school. This approach encourages female students to pursue further studies with greater independence and security. The scheme also supports the state's environmental goals by encouraging the adoption of electric vehicles among the youth.
targetBeneficiaries: The scheme targets the highest-scoring male student and the highest-scoring female student from each government higher secondary school in Madhya Pradesh who have successfully passed the Class 12 board examinations in the given academic year.
ageCriteria: Not officially specified
incomeCriteria: Not officially specified
residencyCriteria: The applicant must be a bona fide resident of Madhya Pradesh.
categoryCriteria: The scheme is open to students from all social categories, including General, Scheduled Castes (SC), Scheduled Tribes (ST), and Other Backward Classes (OBC).
occupationCriteria: The applicant must be a regular student enrolled in and having passed Class 12 from a government higher secondary school in Madhya Pradesh.
otherConditions: The student must have secured the highest marks among all male or all female students in their specific school in the Class 12 board examinations conducted by the Madhya Pradesh Board of Secondary Education.
financialBenefits: The government provides financial assistance directly into the beneficiary's bank account for the purchase of a scooty. The amount is intended to cover the ex-showroom price, registration, and insurance of the vehicle.
nonFinancialBenefits: The scheme provides students with an independent and reliable mode of transportation, which enhances their access to educational institutions and other opportunities. It also serves as a form of public recognition for academic achievement, boosting student morale and motivation.
maximumBenefitAmount: Amount as per latest government notification
benefitFrequency: This is a one-time benefit provided to the eligible student after the declaration of their Class 12 board examination results.
requiredDocuments: Aadhaar card of the student for identity and address verification. | Mark sheet of the Class 12 board examination. | Domicile certificate confirming residency in Madhya Pradesh. | Proof of admission to a government higher secondary school. | Bank account passbook of the student for direct benefit transfer. | Recent passport-sized photographs of the applicant. | A valid mobile number for communication purposes.
modeOfApplication: The application process is primarily managed online through the state's official education portal and is facilitated by the respective school authorities.
stepByStepProcess: The School Education Department prepares a list of eligible toppers from each government school based on official board exam results. | School principals verify the details of the identified meritorious students. | Eligible students are notified and guided to register on the designated government portal. | The student logs in to the portal and fills out the application form with all necessary details. | All required documents must be scanned and uploaded as per the specified format. | The student provides their bank account details for the financial assistance transfer. | The submitted application is verified by the designated authorities at the district level. | Upon final approval, the sanctioned amount is transferred directly to the student's bank account to purchase the scooty.
processingTime: As per departmental timelines after result declaration
approvalAuthority: The final approval is granted by the School Education Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh, following verification at the school and district levels.
importantDates: The selection and application process commences following the annual declaration of the Class 12 board examination results.

overview:
The Gaon Ki Beti Yojana is a scholarship scheme initiated by the Government of Madhya Pradesh to promote higher education among girls from rural areas. The primary aim is to provide financial assistance to meritorious female students who have successfully passed their 12th standard examination with a first division while studying in a village school. This initiative acknowledges and addresses the financial barriers that often prevent talented rural girls from pursuing undergraduate studies.

By offering a regular scholarship, the government seeks to encourage girls to enroll in higher education courses, thereby reducing the gender and urban-rural gap in the education sector. The scheme serves as a significant tool for women's empowerment, enabling beneficiaries to become self-reliant and contribute effectively to the socio-economic fabric of the state.
objectives:
The main objective of the scheme is to financially support talented girls from villages to continue their education at the graduate level without interruption. It aims to significantly increase the Gross Enrollment Ratio (GER) of female students in higher education institutions across Madhya Pradesh and to prevent dropouts after the completion of senior secondary education.

Furthermore, the yojana endeavors to empower rural women through education, fostering academic excellence and creating a more educated and skilled female population. It motivates students to excel in their studies by recognizing and rewarding their academic achievements, ultimately preparing them for better employment opportunities and a brighter future.
targetBeneficiaries: The scheme is designed for meritorious female students who are permanent residents of a rural area in Madhya Pradesh and have secured a first division (60% or more marks) in their 12th standard board examinations from a school located in a village.
ageCriteria: Not officially specified
incomeCriteria: There is no specific income limit for the applicant's family.
residencyCriteria: The applicant must be a permanent resident of a rural area within Madhya Pradesh.
categoryCriteria: The scheme is open to eligible female students from all social categories.
otherConditions: The applicant must have passed the 12th standard examination with a first division or equivalent grade. She must be enrolled as a regular student in a government or government-aided higher education institution for an undergraduate degree course.
financialBenefits: Eligible students receive a scholarship of INR 500 per month for a duration of ten months in an academic year.
nonFinancialBenefits: The scheme promotes continued education, enhances career prospects, and provides official recognition for academic merit, thereby boosting the confidence and social standing of the beneficiaries.
maximumBenefitAmount: INR 5000 per academic year.
benefitFrequency: The scholarship is disbursed annually and is renewable for the entire duration of the undergraduate course, subject to the student meeting the renewal criteria.
requiredDocuments: Applicant's Aadhar card is required for identity verification. | The domicile certificate of Madhya Pradesh is mandatory. | A certificate from the Gram Sarpanch confirming the applicant's residency in the village is needed. | The mark sheet of the 12th standard examination serves as proof of academic eligibility. | Proof of admission to a regular undergraduate course in a recognized institution is required. | The front page of the applicant's bank account passbook is necessary for fund transfer. | A recent passport-sized photograph of the applicant must be provided. | The Samagra ID of the applicant and her family is also required.
modeOfApplication: Applications are accepted online through the State Scholarship Portal 2.0 of the Government of Madhya Pradesh.
stepByStepProcess: Visit the official Madhya Pradesh State Scholarship Portal 2.0. | Complete the new user registration process to create a unique login ID and password. | Log in to the portal using the newly created credentials. | Navigate to the scheme section and select the Gaon Ki Beti Yojana application form. | Fill out the application form with all the required personal, academic, and bank details. | Upload scanned copies of all the mandatory documents in the prescribed format. | Carefully review the entire application form for any errors before final submission. | Submit the completed application and save a copy of the final submission receipt for future reference.
processingTime: Not officially specified
approvalAuthority: The application is verified by the principal of the concerned educational institution and subsequently approved by the designated authority in the Department of Higher Education.
importantDates: The application window typically opens at the beginning of each academic session. Students are advised to check the official scholarship portal for precise deadlines and notifications.

overview:
Pratibha Kiran Yojana is a scholarship scheme initiated by the Government of Madhya Pradesh to promote higher education among meritorious girl students from economically weaker sections. The scheme specifically targets girls residing in urban areas who belong to families living below the poverty line (BPL).

Under this yojana, financial assistance is provided to eligible students who have secured a first division in their 12th standard examination. The assistance aims to support them in pursuing undergraduate studies in various fields, thereby preventing dropouts due to financial constraints and encouraging them to achieve their academic aspirations.
objectives:
The primary objective of the Pratibha Kiran Yojana is to financially support and encourage meritorious girl students from urban BPL families to enroll in higher education courses. The scheme aims to enhance the gross enrollment ratio of girls in colleges and universities.

By providing this scholarship, the government seeks to empower young women through education, making them self-reliant and enabling their participation in the state's economic development. It also endeavors to bridge the gender gap in higher education and ensure that poverty does not become a barrier to academic excellence for deserving female students.
targetBeneficiaries: The scheme is targeted towards meritorious girl students from families classified under the Below Poverty Line (BPL) category, who are permanent residents of urban areas in Madhya Pradesh.
ageCriteria: Not officially specified
incomeCriteria: The family of the applicant must be registered under the Below Poverty Line (BPL) category and possess a valid BPL ration card.
residencyCriteria: The applicant must be a permanent resident of an urban area within the state of Madhya Pradesh.
categoryCriteria: The scheme is open to girl students from all social categories who fulfill the other specified eligibility conditions.
otherConditions: The applicant must have successfully passed the 12th standard examination with a first division, securing 60% or more marks. She must be enrolled for regular study in a government or government-aided institution for an undergraduate degree course.
financialBenefits: Eligible students receive a monthly scholarship to cover educational expenses. The amount is provided for 10 months in an academic year throughout the duration of the course.
nonFinancialBenefits: The scheme promotes educational continuity, empowers young women from underprivileged backgrounds, and fosters an environment of academic achievement.
maximumBenefitAmount: An assistance of INR 500 per month is provided for conventional courses like BA, BSc, BCom. For technical and medical education courses, the assistance is INR 750 per month.
benefitFrequency: The scholarship amount is disbursed to the beneficiary's bank account annually for each academic year of the course.
requiredDocuments: Applicant's Aadhar card is required for identity verification. | A valid Samagra ID of the applicant or the family is necessary. | The mark sheet of the 12th standard examination is mandatory to prove first division. | A valid Below Poverty Line (BPL) card issued by the competent authority is required. | The applicant must provide a domicile certificate of Madhya Pradesh. | Proof of admission to a recognized higher education institution is needed. | Bank account passbook's front page showing account details is required for fund transfer. | A recent passport-sized photograph of the applicant must be provided.
modeOfApplication: Applications for the scheme are accepted online through the official State Scholarship Portal 2.0 of the Madhya Pradesh Government.
stepByStepProcess: Visit the official Madhya Pradesh State Scholarship Portal 2.0. | Complete the new user registration process by providing Aadhar and Samagra ID details. | Log in to the portal using the credentials received after registration. | Search for Pratibha Kiran Yojana and select the 'Apply' option. | Fill out the online application form with accurate personal, academic, and bank information. | Upload scanned copies of all the required documents in the specified format. | Carefully review the entire application for any errors before final submission. | Submit the application form and save or print the acknowledgement receipt for future records.
processingTime: The processing and disbursement timeline is dependent on the verification conducted by the applicant's educational institution and the final approval by the department.
approvalAuthority: The application is verified at the institutional level and the final sanction and disbursement are managed by the Higher Education Department.
importantDates: The application window typically opens at the beginning of the academic session. Students are advised to check the official scholarship portal for the exact schedule and deadlines each year.

schemeName: MP Post Matric Scholarship Scheme for SC/ST/OBC Students
overview:
The Post Matric Scholarship Scheme is a centrally sponsored initiative implemented by the Government of Madhya Pradesh to provide financial assistance to students belonging to Scheduled Castes (SC), Scheduled Tribes (ST), and Other Backward Classes (OBC). The primary aim is to support their education beyond the matriculation or secondary school stage, enabling them to pursue higher studies in recognized institutions across the country.

The scheme covers various educational expenses, including the reimbursement of compulsory non-refundable fees, maintenance allowances, and other allowances for books and academic materials. By mitigating the financial burden on families, the scholarship encourages students from marginalized communities to enroll in and complete their higher education, thereby enhancing their prospects for employment and socio-economic development. The entire application and disbursement process is managed online through the State Scholarship Portal 2.0 for transparency and efficiency.
objectives: The principal objective of the MP Post Matric Scholarship Scheme is to significantly reduce the dropout rate of SC, ST, and OBC students at the post-secondary level of education. It seeks to ensure that financial constraints do not become a barrier for meritorious students from these communities in pursuing academic and professional courses of their choice. The scheme aims to promote educational equity, improve the gross enrollment ratio in higher education among these categories, and empower them to achieve their full potential, contributing to their overall social and economic upliftment.
targetBeneficiaries: The scheme targets students who are permanent residents of Madhya Pradesh and belong to the Scheduled Castes (SC), Scheduled Tribes (ST), or Other Backward Classes (OBC) categories. It is applicable to those who are pursuing recognized post-matriculation or post-secondary courses in government or private institutions.
ageCriteria: Not officially specified
incomeCriteria: For students belonging to Scheduled Castes (SC) and Scheduled Tribes (ST), the annual family income from all sources must not exceed INR 6,00,000. For students from Other Backward Classes (OBC), the annual family income limit is INR 3,00,000.
residencyCriteria: The applicant must be a domicile or a permanent resident of Madhya Pradesh.
categoryCriteria: The applicant must belong to the Scheduled Caste (SC), Scheduled Tribe (ST), or Other Backward Classes (OBC) as notified by the Government of Madhya Pradesh.
otherConditions: The applicant must be enrolled in a full-time post-matriculation course at a recognized educational institution. Students pursuing studies through correspondence or distance education are also eligible under specific conditions. Only two children from the same family can avail the benefits of this scholarship.
financialBenefits: The scheme provides for reimbursement of compulsory non-refundable fees, a monthly maintenance allowance for hostellers and day scholars, book allowances for students pursuing specific professional courses, and allowances for study tours and thesis typing or printing charges.
nonFinancialBenefits: The scheme facilitates continued access to higher education, leading to enhanced employability and better career opportunities. It promotes social inclusion and contributes to the overall educational and economic empowerment of the targeted communities.
maximumBenefitAmount: Amount as per latest government notification
benefitFrequency: Annually
requiredDocuments: Aadhaar card of the applicant. | Samagra ID of the applicant and their family. | Digital caste certificate issued by a competent authority. | Digital domicile certificate of Madhya Pradesh. | Annual family income certificate issued by a competent authority. | Mark sheet of the last qualifying board or university examination. | Proof of admission and fee receipt from the current institution. | Bank account passbook showing the applicant's name, account number, and IFSC code.
modeOfApplication: Online through the State Scholarship Portal 2.0.
stepByStepProcess: Visit the official Madhya Pradesh State Scholarship Portal 2.0. | Register as a new user by entering your Samagra ID and completing the e-KYC process using Aadhaar. | Log in to the portal using the generated user ID and password. | Select the relevant Post Matric Scholarship Scheme and fill out the application form with all required personal, academic, and bank details. | Upload scanned copies of all necessary documents in the prescribed format and size. | Review the application form for any errors and submit it electronically. | Take a printout of the submitted application form and submit it along with physical copies of the documents to your educational institution for verification.
processingTime: The processing duration is subject to timely verification of the application by the educational institution and subsequent approval by the respective district-level departmental office.
approvalAuthority: The scholarship is sanctioned by the designated sanctioning officer of the concerned district office of the Tribal Affairs, Scheduled Caste Development, or Backward Classes & Minorities Welfare Department.
importantDates: The application window typically opens at the start of the academic year. Specific deadlines for application submission and institutional verification are announced annually on the official portal.

overview:
The Kanya Saksharta Protsahan Yojana is a key initiative of the School Education Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh, aimed at enhancing educational opportunities for girls. The scheme is specifically designed to encourage continued education among female students from rural areas where government schools for middle, high, or higher secondary education are not available within their own village.

Under this scheme, a one-time financial incentive is provided to eligible girl students upon their admission into Class 6, Class 9, and Class 11 in a school located in a different village or city. This financial support helps mitigate travel and other educational expenses, thereby motivating parents to ensure their daughters do not drop out and are able to complete their schooling.
objectives:
The primary objective of the scheme is to increase the enrollment and retention rate of girl students, particularly at crucial transition points in their academic journey. It endeavors to reduce the gender disparity in education and improve the overall female literacy rate in the state.

By providing targeted financial assistance, the government aims to overcome the economic and geographical barriers that often prevent girls from rural backgrounds from pursuing further education. The scheme plays a vital role in empowering girls through education, contributing to their personal development and future self-reliance.
targetBeneficiaries: The scheme targets girl students who are permanent residents of rural areas in Madhya Pradesh. Beneficiaries are girls who have passed Class 5, Class 8, or Class 10 and are seeking admission in the next grade, but do not have a government-operated middle school, high school, or higher secondary school, respectively, within the geographical limits of their native village.
ageCriteria: Not officially specified, however, the student must be enrolled in the respective class to be eligible for the benefit.
incomeCriteria: There is no specific income limit defined for this scheme. It is accessible to all eligible girl students irrespective of their family's annual income.
residencyCriteria: The applicant must be a permanent resident of a rural area within the state of Madhya Pradesh.
categoryCriteria: The scheme is available to all eligible girl students from every social category, including Scheduled Caste (SC), Scheduled Tribe (ST), Other Backward Classes (OBC), and General.
otherConditions: The student must be enrolled in a government or government-aided school. The primary condition for eligibility is the non-availability of a government middle school, high school, or higher secondary school in her village of residence.
financialBenefits: A one-time financial incentive of INR 500 is provided upon admission to Class 6, INR 1000 upon admission to Class 9, and INR 3000 upon admission to Class 11.
nonFinancialBenefits: The scheme promotes continued education, helps reduce the school dropout rate among girls, and contributes to increasing the female literacy rate and empowerment in the state.
maximumBenefitAmount: The maximum financial assistance provided to a single beneficiary under this scheme is INR 3000 upon taking admission in Class 11.
benefitFrequency: The financial benefit is provided as a one-time grant at each eligible stage of schooling, specifically upon successful admission to Class 6, Class 9, and Class 11.
requiredDocuments: The student's Samagra ID is mandatory for the application. | Aadhaar card of the student for identity verification. | Mark sheet of the previously passed class, such as 5th, 8th, or 10th. | A certificate from the Gram Panchayat Secretary or Sarpanch confirming the non-availability of the required school level in the student's village. | Proof of admission to the new school located in another village or town. | Bank account details seeded with Aadhaar for direct benefit transfer. | Domicile certificate of Madhya Pradesh. | A recent passport-sized photograph of the student.
modeOfApplication: Applications for the scheme are accepted only through online mode via the Madhya Pradesh State Scholarship Portal 2.0.
stepByStepProcess: The eligible student must first register on the Madhya Pradesh State Scholarship Portal 2.0. | Complete the online application form with accurate personal, academic, and bank details. | Upload clear, scanned copies of all the mandatory documents as specified. | Review the filled application form carefully to ensure all information is correct before final submission. | Submit the application form through the portal. | The application will be verified by the Principal or Headmaster of the concerned school. | Following school-level verification, the application is forwarded to the District Education Officer for final approval. | Upon approval, the financial assistance amount is directly transferred to the beneficiary's bank account.
processingTime: Not officially specified. The processing timeline generally aligns with the academic calendar and departmental verification schedules.
approvalAuthority: The primary verification is done by the school Principal, and the final approval is granted by the District Education Officer (DEO).
departmentHelpline: For scheme-related assistance, applicants can contact the scholarship portal helpline at 0755-2660063.
stateHelpline: For general inquiries or to register a grievance, the statewide CM Helpline number 181 can be used.
importantDates: The application period typically commences at the beginning of each academic session. The exact start and end dates for applications are announced annually on the State Scholarship Portal.

overview:
The Super 5000 Scholarship, officially known as the Mukhyamantri Medhavi Vidhyarthi Yojana, is a flagship initiative of the Government of Madhya Pradesh. The scheme is designed to provide comprehensive financial assistance to meritorious students of the state for pursuing higher education in various undergraduate programs at recognized institutions across India. Under this program, the government bears the full tuition fee of eligible students who secure admission in specified courses, thereby ensuring that financial constraints do not hinder their academic pursuits.

This initiative aims to foster academic excellence by rewarding high-performing students who have excelled in their 12th-grade examinations. It covers a wide range of disciplines including engineering, medical, law, management, and other general undergraduate courses. By removing the financial barriers to quality higher education, the scheme empowers talented youth to achieve their full potential and contribute effectively to the development of the state and the nation.
objectives:
The primary objective of the scheme is to ensure that meritorious students from economically weaker sections of society in Madhya Pradesh are not deprived of higher education opportunities due to a lack of financial resources. It seeks to encourage and motivate students to perform exceptionally well in their secondary board examinations by offering a clear pathway to funded university education.

The scheme also aims to enhance the Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in higher education within the state. By facilitating access to premier educational institutions, the government intends to create a skilled and educated workforce, promote social equity, and support the intellectual development of its young citizens, ultimately contributing to the overall socio-economic progress of Madhya Pradesh.
targetBeneficiaries: The scheme targets meritorious students who are permanent residents of Madhya Pradesh and have successfully passed their 12th-grade examinations from either the Madhya Pradesh Board of Secondary Education (MPBSE) or the Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE)/Indian Certificate of Secondary Education (ICSE) boards.
ageCriteria: Not officially specified
incomeCriteria: The annual family income of the student from all sources should not exceed INR 6,00,000. For students whose parents have passed away, the income limit is INR 8,00,000.
residencyCriteria: The applicant must be a bonafide and permanent resident of the state of Madhya Pradesh.
categoryCriteria: The scheme is available to eligible students from all social categories, including General, Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, and Other Backward Classes.
otherConditions: Students must have secured 70% or more marks in the 12th standard examination conducted by the Madhya Pradesh Board of Secondary Education, or 85% or more marks in the CBSE/ICSE board examination. The student must also have secured admission in a designated undergraduate course at a government or recognized private institution.
financialBenefits: The scheme provides full reimbursement of the tuition fee for the entire duration of the approved undergraduate course. This includes admission fees and other mandatory fees charged by the educational institution.
nonFinancialBenefits: The scheme provides students with the opportunity to pursue their chosen field of study in reputed institutions without financial worry, thereby enabling better career prospects and fostering academic confidence.
maximumBenefitAmount: The entire course fee for government institutions is covered. For private institutions, the fee reimbursement is subject to the limits determined by the State Fee Regulatory Authority.
benefitFrequency: The financial benefit is disbursed on an annual basis directly to the institution or the student, and is renewable each year subject to the student's satisfactory academic performance and promotion to the next academic year.
requiredDocuments: Aadhar card of the applicant. | Samagra ID of the student's family. | Mark sheets of Class 10th and 12th examinations. | Domicile certificate confirming residency in Madhya Pradesh. | Annual family income certificate issued by a competent authority. | Proof of admission letter or fee receipt from the educational institution. | Bank account passbook of the student for Direct Benefit Transfer. | Recent passport-sized photograph of the applicant.
modeOfApplication: Applications must be submitted online through the official State Scholarship Portal 2.0 of the Madhya Pradesh government.
stepByStepProcess: Visit the official State Scholarship Portal 2.0 for Madhya Pradesh. | Complete the user registration process by providing your Samagra ID and Aadhar details. | Log in to the portal using the credentials created during registration. | Select the Mukhyamantri Medhavi Vidhyarthi Yojana from the list of available schemes. | Fill out the online application form with accurate personal, academic, and institutional information. | Upload clear, scanned copies of all the mandatory documents in the specified format. | Carefully review the entire application form for any errors or omissions before final submission. | Submit the application and save or print the acknowledgement receipt for future reference.
processingTime: The processing and verification time varies, as it is dependent on the validation conducted by the concerned educational institution and the final approval by the department.
approvalAuthority: The application is first verified by the Nodal Officer at the student's institution and subsequently approved by the sanctioning authority at the Department of Higher Education.
importantDates: The application window typically opens after the declaration of 12th board examination results and aligns with the higher education admission schedule. Specific dates are announced annually on the official portal.

overview:
The Swachh Vidyalaya Abhiyan is a state-level implementation of the national campaign aimed at ensuring that every school in Madhya Pradesh has access to separate and functional toilets for boys and girls. The initiative is a critical component of creating a safe, healthy, and supportive learning environment for all students. It focuses not only on the construction of sanitation infrastructure but also on promoting behavioral changes related to hygiene practices like handwashing with soap.

The scheme operates under the broader framework of the Samagra Shiksha Abhiyan and aligns with the objectives of the Swachh Bharat Mission. Its successful implementation is crucial for improving key educational indicators, including enrollment, attendance, and retention rates, particularly for female students who are disproportionately affected by the lack of proper sanitation facilities. The School Management Committees (SMCs) are empowered to oversee the construction and maintenance of these facilities, ensuring community ownership and sustainability.
objectives:
The primary objective of the scheme is to provide universal access to separate, well-maintained, and functional toilets for both boys and girls in all government schools across the state. It also aims to ensure the availability of safe and adequate drinking water and handwashing stations to inculcate essential hygiene practices among students from an early age.

Furthermore, the scheme seeks to foster a culture of cleanliness and sanitation within the entire school ecosystem, involving students, teachers, and the local community. By improving the physical infrastructure and promoting hygienic behaviors, the initiative intends to reduce the incidence of waterborne and sanitation-related diseases, thereby enhancing the overall health and well-being of the student population and reducing student absenteeism.
targetBeneficiaries: The direct beneficiaries of this scheme are all students enrolled in government and government-aided schools within Madhya Pradesh. Special emphasis is placed on ensuring benefits for girl students, for whom the lack of private and secure sanitation facilities is a major barrier to education. The scheme also indirectly benefits teachers, school staff, and the wider community by promoting a healthier environment.
ageCriteria: Not applicable to individual beneficiaries as the scheme targets educational institutions.
incomeCriteria: Not applicable. The scheme is implemented in all government schools irrespective of the economic background of the students.
residencyCriteria: The scheme is applicable to all government schools located within the geographical boundaries of Madhya Pradesh.
categoryCriteria: The scheme covers all government schools, including primary, middle, high, and higher secondary schools, in both rural and urban areas.
otherConditions: The primary eligibility is for a school to be a government or government-aided institution. Priority is accorded to schools that currently lack any toilet facilities, have non-functional toilets, or have a common toilet for boys and girls.
financialBenefits: Financial assistance is provided to schools through their School Management Committees for the construction of new toilet units, urinals, and handwashing stations. Funds are also allocated for major and minor repairs of existing sanitation infrastructure and for ensuring a regular water supply to these facilities.
nonFinancialBenefits: The scheme leads to a cleaner and more hygienic school environment, which reduces the spread of diseases. It significantly improves school attendance and reduces dropout rates, especially among adolescent girls. The initiative also fosters positive behavioral changes regarding sanitation and hygiene among students, which they carry into their homes and communities.
maximumBenefitAmount: Amount as per latest government notification
benefitFrequency: A one-time grant is typically provided for new construction, with recurring annual grants allocated for operation and maintenance activities.
requiredDocuments: A formal application and proposal submitted by the School Management Committee. | A resolution passed by the School Management Committee detailing the requirement for sanitation facilities. | Proof of land availability within the school premises for the proposed construction. | A detailed engineering estimate and plan for the construction or repair work. | Bank account details of the School Management Committee registered for the scheme. | Photographic evidence of the existing sanitation status or the proposed site of construction. | A certificate from the school headmaster confirming the need for the facilities.
modeOfApplication: Offline. Proposals are prepared by the School Management Committee and submitted through the appropriate administrative channel.
stepByStepProcess: The School Management Committee identifies the need for sanitation infrastructure and passes a formal resolution. | The school headmaster prepares a detailed proposal with cost estimates and submits it to the Block Education Officer. | The Block Education Officer verifies the proposal and forwards it to the District Education Office. | The District Project Coordinator for Samagra Shiksha Abhiyan reviews the proposal for compliance with scheme guidelines and technical feasibility. | Upon approval by the district-level committee, the proposal is sanctioned. | Funds are electronically transferred to the designated bank account of the School Management Committee. | The School Management Committee executes the construction or repair work as per the approved plan. | Upon completion, utilization certificates and completion reports are submitted to the district office.
processingTime: Not officially specified
approvalAuthority: The primary approval authority is the District Project Coordinator or the District Collector, based on the scale of the project.
applicationLink: Not applicable as the application process is offline through departmental channels.
departmentHelpline: Contact information is available on the official website of the School Education Department.
importantDates: The scheme is ongoing. Proposals from schools are typically collected and reviewed at the beginning of each financial year.

overview:
The Kasturba Gandhi Balika Vidyalaya (KGBV) scheme is a centrally sponsored initiative implemented by the Government of Madhya Pradesh to establish residential schools at the upper primary level for girls belonging predominantly to disadvantaged sections of society. These schools are established in Educationally Backward Blocks (EBBs) where female rural literacy is below the national average and the gender gap in literacy is above the national average.

The scheme aims to provide a safe, secure, and conducive learning environment to bridge gender and social category gaps in education. These residential schools offer free boarding and lodging facilities, along with tuition, textbooks, uniforms, and other necessary materials, ensuring that girls from marginalized communities can complete their elementary education without financial constraints. Many of these schools have also been upgraded to the secondary and senior secondary levels to ensure continuity of education.
objectives: The primary objective of the Kasturba Gandhi Balika Vidyalaya scheme is to ensure access to and retention in quality education for girls from Scheduled Castes (SC), Scheduled Tribes (ST), Other Backward Classes (OBC), minority communities, and Below Poverty Line (BPL) families. It specifically targets out-of-school girls in difficult-to-reach areas. The scheme endeavors to reduce the dropout rate among girls, facilitate their smooth transition from elementary to secondary education, and empower them with life skills and vocational training to enhance their future prospects and promote self-reliance.
targetBeneficiaries: The scheme targets girls in the age group of 10-14 who are out of school, including dropouts and those who have never been enrolled. A minimum of 75 percent of the enrollment is reserved for girls from SC, ST, OBC, and minority communities, while the remaining 25 percent is allocated to girls from families living Below the Poverty Line.
ageCriteria: Admissions are primarily for girls entering Class VI, typically in the age group of 10 to 12 years, with provisions for older out-of-school girls.
incomeCriteria: Priority is given to girls from families designated as Below the Poverty Line (BPL).
residencyCriteria: The applicant must be a resident of the specific Educationally Backward Block where the Kasturba Gandhi Balika Vidyalaya is located.
categoryCriteria: A minimum of 75% of seats are reserved for girls belonging to Scheduled Caste (SC), Scheduled Tribe (ST), Other Backward Classes (OBC), and minority communities.
otherConditions: Priority is given to girls who are orphans, come from single-parent households, or are otherwise in difficult circumstances and are unable to continue their education.
financialBenefits: The scheme does not provide direct financial transfers to the beneficiaries; instead, it covers all educational and living expenses at the residential school.
nonFinancialBenefits: Beneficiaries receive free residential schooling which includes boarding, lodging, uniforms, textbooks, stationery, medical care, and bridge courses for age-appropriate enrollment. It also includes provisions for skill development, vocational training, and self-defense classes.
maximumBenefitAmount: Not officially specified
benefitFrequency: Benefits are provided continuously throughout the duration of the student's enrollment in the Vidyalaya.
requiredDocuments: Birth certificate or an equivalent document for age verification. | Aadhar card of the applicant and her parent or guardian. | Proof of residence such as a ration card or domicile certificate. | Caste certificate issued by a competent authority for SC, ST, or OBC applicants. | Income certificate or BPL card to verify the family's economic status. | Transfer certificate from the previous school if the student was enrolled elsewhere. | Passport-sized photographs of the applicant. | A declaration confirming the applicant is an out-of-school child, where applicable.
modeOfApplication: The application process is conducted offline through prescribed forms.
stepByStepProcess: Obtain the official application form from the nearest Kasturba Gandhi Balika Vidyalaya, Block Education Office, or District Education Office. | Complete the application form with all the required personal, family, and academic details. | Attach self-attested photocopies of all the mandatory documents as listed in the guidelines. | Submit the filled-in application form and attached documents to the Headmaster of the concerned KGBV or the Block Resource Coordinator before the deadline. | The received applications are verified and scrutinized by a designated selection committee at the block level. | A merit list of eligible candidates is prepared based on the reservation criteria and prioritization norms. | Selected candidates and their guardians are formally notified about the admission and the schedule for joining the school.
processingTime: The admission process is typically completed before the commencement of the new academic session annually.
approvalAuthority: The selection of beneficiaries is approved by a committee constituted at the district or block level, typically headed by the District Collector or a designated education officer.
departmentHelpline: For specific queries, contact the office of the District Education Officer (DEO) in the respective district.
importantDates: The admission drive and application submission period generally occurs between April and June each year, prior to the start of the new academic session.

schemeName: Post Matric Scholarship Scheme for ST Students
overview:
The Post Matric Scholarship Scheme for ST Students is a Centrally Sponsored Scheme implemented by the Government of Madhya Pradesh to provide financial assistance to students from the Scheduled Tribe community. The scheme aims to support their educational pursuits for studies undertaken after the matriculation or secondary school stage, encompassing a wide range of courses from intermediate to postgraduate and professional levels in recognized institutions across the state and country. This financial aid is intended to cover essential academic expenses, thereby reducing the financial burden on families and encouraging students to continue their education without interruption.

The primary objective is to enable a significant number of ST students to obtain higher education and secure better employment opportunities. By providing scholarships that cover tuition fees, maintenance allowances, and other compulsory non-refundable fees, the scheme seeks to minimize the dropout rate among ST students at the post-secondary level.
