Furthermore, the scheme endeavors to remove social barriers and stigmas associated with the marriage of disabled individuals. By doing so, it promotes equality and provides a platform for their social rehabilitation, ultimately contributing to their overall well-being and happiness.
targetBeneficiaries: The scheme targets married couples where either the bride, the groom, or both partners are persons with a disability of 40 percent or more. The beneficiaries must be permanent residents of Madhya Pradesh and meet the prescribed age and income criteria.
ageCriteria: The bride must have completed 18 years of age, and the groom must have completed 21 years of age at the time of marriage.
incomeCriteria: The applicant, or the couple jointly, should not be in the category of income tax payees.
residencyCriteria: The disabled person in the couple must be a permanent resident of Madhya Pradesh.
categoryCriteria: The scheme is applicable to individuals with a disability of 40 percent or more, as certified by a competent medical authority and possessing a valid disability certificate (UDID card).
otherConditions: The marriage must be the first marriage for both the bride and the groom. The application for assistance must be submitted within one year of the date of marriage registration.
financialBenefits: A one-time financial incentive is provided to the eligible married couple. The amount is directly transferred to the joint bank account of the couple.
nonFinancialBenefits: The scheme promotes social acceptance, inclusion, and the overall rehabilitation of persons with disabilities within the community.
benefitFrequency: One-time assistance per eligible couple.
requiredDocuments: A valid disability certificate (UDID Card) indicating 40% or more disability. | The official marriage registration certificate issued by the competent authority. | Proof of age for both the bride and groom, such as a birth certificate or Class 10 marksheet. | Domicile certificate of Madhya Pradesh for the disabled partner. | An affidavit from both partners declaring that this is their first marriage. | A joint bank account passbook in the name of the couple. | A joint photograph of the married couple. | Aadhaar cards of both the bride and the groom.
modeOfApplication: The application process is conducted online through the official portal of the Social Justice & Disabled Welfare Department.
stepByStepProcess: Visit the official website of the Social Justice & Disabled Welfare Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh. | Register as a new user on the portal or log in with existing credentials. | Navigate to the section for the Disabled Marriage Assistance Scheme. | Fill out the online application form accurately with all the required personal and marital details. | Upload clear, scanned copies of all the mandatory documents in the prescribed format. | Review the completed application form for any errors before final submission. | Upon submission, the application is forwarded to the concerned District's Social Justice office for verification. | After successful verification and approval, the financial assistance is sanctioned and disbursed to the couple's joint bank account.
processingTime: Not officially specified
approvalAuthority: The sanctioning authority for the scheme is the Joint Director or Deputy Director of the Social Justice & Disabled Welfare Department at the district level.
importantDates: Applications for the scheme are accepted throughout the year. However, the application must be submitted within one year from the date of marriage.

overview:
The Mukhyamantri Kalyani Vivah Sahayata Yojana is a social welfare initiative by the Government of Madhya Pradesh aimed at promoting the remarriage of widows, referred to as 'Kalyani' under the scheme. This program provides one-time financial assistance to eligible widows to encourage them to start a new life and integrate into mainstream society. The scheme intends to address the social stigma and financial hardships often faced by widows, thereby empowering them and ensuring their social security and well-being.

The implementation of this scheme is managed by the Social Justice & Disabled Welfare Department. It ensures that the financial aid is disbursed directly to the beneficiary's bank account after the solemnization and legal registration of the marriage. This initiative represents a significant step towards social reform, seeking to create a more supportive and inclusive environment for widows in the state.
objectives:
The primary objective of the scheme is to provide social and financial security to widows by encouraging their remarriage. It aims to empower them to lead a dignified life with respect and to challenge and change the orthodox societal norms and prejudices associated with widowhood. By offering financial support, the government seeks to facilitate the establishment of the new household for the remarried couple.

Furthermore, the scheme intends to promote a progressive mindset within the community regarding widow remarriage. It strives to ensure that widows are not subjected to social exclusion or economic dependency, thus contributing to their overall empowerment and social upliftment. The program serves as a support system that enables widows to make independent life choices.
targetBeneficiaries: The scheme targets widows (Kalyani) who are permanent residents of Madhya Pradesh and are remarrying.
ageCriteria: The bride (Kalyani) must be between 18 and 45 years of age at the time of her remarriage. The groom must have completed the legal age for marriage, which is 21 years.
incomeCriteria: The applicant widow (Kalyani) must not be an income tax payee.
residencyCriteria: The applicant widow must be a native and permanent resident of Madhya Pradesh.
categoryCriteria: The scheme is available to eligible widows from all social categories without any caste or creed-based restrictions.
occupationCriteria: The applicant should not be employed in any government service or be a recipient of benefits from any government-aided institution.
otherConditions: The remarriage must be legally registered under the relevant marriage act. The marriage must be the first marriage for the groom. The applicant should not be receiving any family pension from the government.
financialBenefits: A one-time financial assistance grant is provided to the eligible widow upon her remarriage.
nonFinancialBenefits: The scheme offers social recognition and support, promoting the social integration of widows and helping to eliminate stigmas associated with widow remarriage.
benefitFrequency: This is a one-time grant provided after the remarriage is officially registered.
requiredDocuments: Aadhar card of the bride (Kalyani) and the groom. | Samagra ID of the bride is mandatory. | Domicile certificate of Madhya Pradesh for the bride. | Death certificate of the previous husband of the bride. | Age proof for both the bride and the groom, such as a birth certificate or school mark sheet. | A self-declaration by the applicant stating that she is not an income tax payee. | The legally valid marriage registration certificate. | Bank account passbook details of the bride for direct benefit transfer.
modeOfApplication: Applications can be submitted in offline mode to the concerned district office or through the online portal of the Social Justice & Disabled Welfare Department.
stepByStepProcess: The applicant must obtain the prescribed application form from the office of the Deputy Director of the Social Justice Department in their district or download it from the official website. | All required information in the application form must be filled accurately and completely. | All necessary documents must be self-attested and attached to the application form. | The application must be submitted after the marriage has been legally solemnized and registered. | The completed application form along with all documents should be submitted to the office of the Deputy Director/Joint Director of the Social Justice Department in the concerned district. | Officials will conduct a thorough verification of the application and the attached documents. | Upon successful verification and approval by the sanctioning authority, the financial assistance amount is transferred to the beneficiary's bank account.
processingTime: Not officially specified
approvalAuthority: The Deputy Director or Joint Director of the Social Justice & Disabled Welfare Department at the district level is the designated approval and sanctioning authority for the scheme.
importantDates: The application for assistance under the scheme must be submitted within one year from the date of the legal registration of the remarriage.

overview: The Unique Disability ID (UDID) project, initiated by the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities, Government of India, is being implemented across all states, including Madhya Pradesh. The primary objective of this project is to establish a comprehensive national database for Persons with Disabilities (PwDs) and to issue a Unique Disability ID Card to each individual with a disability. This initiative aims to create a transparent and efficient mechanism for the delivery of government benefits and services to PwDs. The UDID card serves as a single, universally accepted document of identity and disability verification for the cardholder across the country. This eliminates the need for carrying multiple documents and ensures that beneficiaries can seamlessly access schemes and services meant for them. The project involves an online portal for registration, renewal, and tracking of applications, thereby enhancing accessibility and accountability in the system. The State Government of Madhya Pradesh, through the Department of Social Justice & Disabled Welfare, facilitates the implementation by coordinating with district medical authorities and other relevant agencies to ensure effective registration and card distribution.
objectives: The main objective of the UDID scheme is to enable Persons with Disabilities to easily access and avail benefits from various government schemes and programs. It aims to create a unified system that avoids duplication of data and ensures the authenticity of the beneficiary. The project seeks to build a real-time national database of PwDs, which is crucial for effective policy planning and implementation at the village, block, district, state, and national levels. By providing a single card, the scheme intends to streamline the process of verification and simplify the lives of PwDs, allowing them to avail benefits related to education, employment, healthcare, and travel concessions without procedural hurdles. It also aims to track the physical and financial progress of beneficiaries on a uniform basis.
targetBeneficiaries: The scheme targets all citizens residing in Madhya Pradesh who are Persons with Disabilities (PwDs) as defined under the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016, and possess a valid Disability Certificate or are eligible for one.
ageCriteria: There is no specific age limit prescribed for obtaining a UDID card.
incomeCriteria: No income criteria is applicable for the issuance of the UDID card. However, income limits may apply for specific schemes that are accessed using the card.
residencyCriteria: The applicant must be a permanent resident of the state of Madhya Pradesh.
categoryCriteria: The applicant must be a person with one of the specified disabilities as certified by a competent medical authority.
otherConditions: The applicant must possess an existing Disability Certificate or must be willing to undergo a medical assessment for the fresh issuance of the certificate and the UDID card.
financialBenefits: Not applicable as this is an identification scheme. The card facilitates access to financial benefits under other designated government schemes.
nonFinancialBenefits: The UDID card serves as a single, universally valid document for identity and disability proof. It streamlines access to various government benefits, schemes, reservations, and concessions in sectors like transport, education, and employment. The card also supports the creation of a reliable national database for policy formulation and implementation.
maximumBenefitAmount: Not applicable
benefitFrequency: Not applicable
requiredDocuments: A recent passport-sized color photograph of the applicant is required. | A scanned image of the applicant's signature or thumb impression must be provided. | Proof of identity such as an Aadhaar card, Voter ID card, or driving license is mandatory. | Proof of residence, which can include an Aadhaar card, ration card, or a domicile certificate, is needed. | The Disability Certificate issued by a competent medical authority is required if already available. | The Aadhaar number of the applicant is essential for registration and verification.
modeOfApplication: Applications can be submitted online through the national Swavlamban portal or offline at District Disability Rehabilitation Centres (DDRC) and designated medical facilities.
stepByStepProcess: The applicant must first register on the official Swavlamban UDID portal. | After registration, log in and fill the online application form with personal, disability, employment, and identity details. | Upload scanned copies of all the required documents, including the photograph and signature. | Review the filled information carefully and submit the application. | The application is electronically forwarded to the Chief Medical and Health Officer (CMHO) of the concerned district for verification. | Following successful verification and medical assessment, the UDID card and disability certificate are generated by the authority. | The applicant can track the application status online and download the e-UDID card and certificate from the portal upon approval.
processingTime: The time for processing varies by district and depends on the verification process by the medical board and administrative authorities.
approvalAuthority: The Chief Medical and Health Officer (CMHO) or a designated medical board at the district level is the primary authority for approval and issuance.
importantDates: This is a continuous scheme with no specified application deadlines.

overview:
The Girl Parent Pension Scheme, also known as Kanya Abhibhavak Pension Yojana, is a social security initiative by the Government of Madhya Pradesh. It is designed to provide financial assistance to elderly parents who have only daughters and no living son. The scheme aims to offer a regular pension to ensure their financial independence and well-being during their old age, thereby challenging the societal preference for a male child.

This scheme recognizes and values the role of daughters and seeks to eliminate gender-based discrimination. By providing a social safety net, the government encourages a positive attitude towards the girl child and supports the principle of a small family norm. The financial support helps parents meet their basic needs without facing dependency or hardship.
objectives: The primary objective of the scheme is to provide monthly financial assistance and social security to elderly couples who only have daughters. It seeks to ensure their dignity and self-reliance in their old age. The scheme also aims to promote the value of the girl child within society, combat gender discrimination, and improve the overall sex ratio in the state by discouraging the preference for sons.
targetBeneficiaries: The scheme targets elderly couples who are permanent residents of Madhya Pradesh and have only daughters, with no living male child. It is specifically intended for parents who are above 60 years of age and belong to families living below the poverty line or are non-income tax payers.
ageCriteria: Both the husband and wife must be 60 years of age or older.
incomeCriteria: The applicants must not be income tax payers and should be listed in the Below Poverty Line (BPL) registry.
residencyCriteria: The couple must be bonafide residents of Madhya Pradesh.
categoryCriteria: Not officially specified
otherConditions: The applicant couple must have only daughter or daughters, with no living biological son.
financialBenefits: Eligible couples receive a combined monthly pension to support their livelihood. The pension amount is credited directly to the joint bank account of the beneficiaries through Direct Benefit Transfer.
nonFinancialBenefits: The scheme provides social security to the beneficiaries and promotes a positive societal change by enhancing the status and value of the girl child.
maximumBenefitAmount: Amount as per latest government notification
benefitFrequency: Monthly
requiredDocuments: Aadhaar card for both husband and wife. | Composite Family ID (Samagra ID). | Proof of residence such as a Voter ID card or Ration Card. | Age verification document for both applicants. | An affidavit from the couple declaring they have only daughters and no son. | Below Poverty Line (BPL) certificate. | Bank account passbook for the joint account. | Recent passport-sized photographs of the couple.
modeOfApplication: Applications can be submitted in offline mode at the respective local government office or online through the Madhya Pradesh Social Security Portal.
stepByStepProcess: Obtain the prescribed application form from the office of the Gram Panchayat, Janpad Panchayat, or Municipal Corporation. | Complete the application form with accurate personal and family details. | Attach self-attested photocopies of all the required documents. | Submit the filled-in application form to the competent authority at the respective local body office. | The submitted application is verified by the officials at the local level. | After successful verification, the application is forwarded to the sanctioning authority for approval. | Upon approval, the pension amount is disbursed monthly to the beneficiary's bank account.
processingTime: The application is typically processed within 30 working days from the date of submission, subject to verification.
approvalAuthority: The sanctioning authority is the Chief Executive Officer of the Janpad Panchayat for rural areas and the Commissioner of the Municipal Corporation or Chief Municipal Officer for urban areas.
departmentHelpline: Contact details are available on the official website of the Social Justice & Disabled Welfare Department.
importantDates: Applications for this scheme are accepted throughout the year as it is an ongoing welfare program.

overview:
The Nishashkt Shiksha Protsahan scheme is a state-sponsored initiative by the Government of Madhya Pradesh aimed at promoting education among students with disabilities. It provides financial assistance in the form of a scholarship to differently-abled students pursuing education from primary level up to post-graduate, technical, and professional courses. The primary goal is to alleviate the financial burden on these students and their families, ensuring they can continue their education without interruption due to economic constraints.

This scheme is an integral part of the state's commitment to fostering inclusive growth and ensuring that persons with disabilities have equal opportunities for development. By supporting their educational aspirations, the government seeks to empower them, enhance their employability, and facilitate their integration into the mainstream of society. The financial aid helps cover essential educational expenses, thereby encouraging enrollment and reducing dropout rates among disabled students across the state.
objectives:
The principal objective of this scheme is to encourage and financially support students with 40% or more disability to pursue and complete their education. It aims to increase the literacy rate and educational attainment level among the disabled population of Madhya Pradesh. The scheme intends to ensure that no disabled student is deprived of education due to a lack of financial resources.

Furthermore, the scheme seeks to bring students with disabilities into the formal education system, thereby promoting social inclusion and equality. By enabling them to access higher and technical education, the initiative works towards making them self-reliant and preparing them for skilled employment, ultimately contributing to their socio-economic upliftment and overall empowerment.
targetBeneficiaries: The scheme is designed for students who are permanent residents of Madhya Pradesh and have a disability of 40% or more, as certified by a competent medical authority. It targets students enrolled in regular courses at government-recognized educational institutions, from primary school to post-graduate, professional, or technical education levels.
ageCriteria: Not officially specified
incomeCriteria: The parents or guardians of the applicant must not be income tax payers. An income certificate is required to be submitted as proof.
residencyCriteria: The applicant must be a permanent resident of Madhya Pradesh.
categoryCriteria: The applicant must be a person with a disability of 40% or more, supported by a valid disability certificate.
occupationCriteria: The applicant must be a regular student enrolled in an educational institution and should not be engaged in any full-time employment.
otherConditions: The student must not be receiving any other scholarship or stipend for the same academic course from any other government department or source. The student must have passed the previous qualifying examination to be eligible for the scholarship for the subsequent year.
financialBenefits: The scheme provides a monthly scholarship, the amount of which varies according to the educational level. Financial assistance is disbursed for different stages, including primary, middle, high school, higher secondary, undergraduate, post-graduate, and professional courses. Additionally, a reader's allowance is provided for blind students pursuing education from class 9 onwards.
nonFinancialBenefits: The scheme promotes educational continuity, enhances self-confidence, and supports the social inclusion of students with disabilities, thereby improving their future prospects for employment and self-reliance.
maximumBenefitAmount: Amount as per latest government notification
benefitFrequency: The scholarship amount is typically disbursed annually directly into the beneficiary's bank account through electronic transfer.
requiredDocuments: Aadhaar card of the student for identity and address verification. | A valid disability certificate issued by a competent medical board indicating 40% or more disability. | A domicile certificate confirming permanent residency in Madhya Pradesh. | An income certificate of the parents or guardian, confirming non-taxpayer status. | Mark sheets of the last examination passed. | Proof of admission or a bonafide student certificate from the educational institution for the current academic year. | Bank account passbook copy with the student's name, account number, and IFSC code. | A recent passport-sized photograph of the applicant.
modeOfApplication: Applications for the scheme are accepted online through the Madhya Pradesh State Scholarship Portal 2.0.
stepByStepProcess: The applicant must first register on the Madhya Pradesh State Scholarship Portal 2.0 to create a user profile. | Log in to the portal using the credentials created during registration. | Navigate to the application form for the Nishashkt Shiksha Protsahan scheme. | Fill out the application form with accurate personal, academic, and bank account information. | Upload scanned copies of all the required documents in the prescribed format and size. | Carefully review the entire application form to ensure all details are correct before final submission. | Submit the application online and download or print the acknowledgement receipt for future reference. | The submitted application will be forwarded to the respective educational institution for verification and further processing.
processingTime: Not officially specified, as it depends on the verification and approval process by the educational institution and the concerned department.
approvalAuthority: The application is verified by the educational institution and sanctioned by the designated officer of the Social Justice & Disabled Welfare Department at the district level.
importantDates: The application window typically opens at the beginning of the academic session each year. Specific deadlines are announced on the official State Scholarship Portal.

schemeName: Civil Service Protsahan Yojana (For Persons with Disabilities)
overview:
The Civil Service Protsahan Yojana is a state-sponsored scheme implemented by the Government of Madhya Pradesh to encourage and support meritorious students with disabilities. The program aims to facilitate their entry into the prestigious administrative services of the state and the nation by providing financial assistance for preparation for competitive examinations conducted by the Madhya Pradesh Public Service Commission (MPPSC) and the Union Public Service Commission (UPSC).

This financial aid is intended to cover expenses related to coaching, study materials, and other preparatory needs, thereby removing economic barriers that may hinder their potential. By offering this support, the government seeks to create a level playing field for aspirants with disabilities, enabling them to compete effectively and secure positions in premier government services. The scheme underscores the state's commitment to the empowerment and inclusive development of persons with disabilities.
objectives: The primary objective of the scheme is to financially empower and motivate students with disabilities to prepare for and succeed in the Civil Service examinations. It aims to increase the representation of persons with disabilities in the administrative machinery of the central and state governments. The scheme endeavors to ensure that no talented and deserving candidate with a disability is deprived of the opportunity to access quality coaching and resources due to financial constraints. It promotes self-reliance, builds confidence, and facilitates the integration of PwD candidates into mainstream governance and nation-building.
targetBeneficiaries: The scheme targets meritorious students with benchmark disabilities who are permanent residents of Madhya Pradesh and are preparing for the Civil Service examinations conducted by the Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) or the Madhya Pradesh Public Service Commission (MPPSC).
ageCriteria: The age of the applicant must conform to the age limit prescribed for the respective civil service examination by the UPSC or MPPSC.
incomeCriteria: The annual family income of the applicant from all sources should not exceed INR 6,00,000.
residencyCriteria: The applicant must be a permanent and bonafide resident of the state of Madhya Pradesh.
categoryCriteria: The applicant must possess a disability certificate indicating 40% or more disability, issued by a competent medical authority.
occupationCriteria: The applicant must be a student preparing for the specified examinations and should not be employed in any government service.
otherConditions: The financial assistance is provided to candidates who have successfully cleared the preliminary examination of the respective civil service. The benefit under this scheme can be availed only once for each stage of the examination (preliminary and main).
financialBenefits: The scheme provides one-time financial assistance to eligible candidates upon clearing different stages of the civil service examinations. The amount is provided for preparation for the Main examination after clearing the Prelims, and for the Interview stage after clearing the Mains.
nonFinancialBenefits: The scheme provides moral and institutional encouragement to PwD aspirants, fostering confidence and promoting their integration into premier government services.
maximumBenefitAmount: Amount as per latest government notification
benefitFrequency: The benefit is provided once for each successful stage of the specified examination.
requiredDocuments: Aadhar card of the applicant. | Valid disability certificate (UDID Card) indicating 40% or more disability. | Domicile certificate of Madhya Pradesh. | Annual family income certificate issued by a competent authority. | Mark sheet and admit card as proof of clearing the preliminary or main examination. | Copy of the first page of the bank passbook showing account number and IFSC code. | Caste certificate, if applicable. | A recent passport-sized photograph of the applicant.
modeOfApplication: Online via the State Scholarship Portal 2.0 and offline through the office of the Deputy/Joint Director, Social Justice and Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities in the respective district.
stepByStepProcess: The applicant must first visit the official State Scholarship Portal of Madhya Pradesh. | Register on the portal by providing the required basic details to create a user profile. | Log in using the generated credentials and locate the Civil Service Protsahan Yojana application form. | Fill in all the required details in the application form accurately. | Upload scanned copies of all the necessary documents as specified in the application. | Review the application thoroughly to ensure all information is correct before final submission. | Submit the completed application form online and keep a copy of the acknowledgment for future reference. | The submitted application will be verified by the officials at the district level before the sanction and disbursal of funds.
processingTime: Not officially specified
approvalAuthority: The Deputy Director/Joint Director of the Social Justice and Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities Department at the district level is the sanctioning authority.
importantDates: Applications are generally accepted throughout the year following the declaration of examination results. Beneficiaries must apply within the stipulated timeframe announced by the department on the official portal.
